title: Supporting the goals and ideals of a Juan Pablo Duarte Day.
plain_summary: This resolution wants to create a special day to honor Juan Pablo Duarte, who helped start the country called the Dominican Republic. Juan Pablo Duarte was an important leader who helped his country become independent in 1844 and believed in democracy. The resolution asks for a national day in the United States to celebrate him and also to honor Dominican Americans who live here. This would be like other special days we have to remember important people and groups in our country.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution supports the goals and ideals of a Juan Pablo Duarte Day.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: The Dominican Republic became independent and free to govern itself on February twenty seventh eighteen forty four. The Dominican Republic was an important place for learning and progress in the Western Hemisphere. It built the first university, hospital, and cathedral in the New World. Juan Pablo Duarte, who is considered the founding father of the Dominican Republic, was born on January twenty sixth eighteen thirteen. In January twenty twenty five, Dominican Americans and people living in the Dominican Republic will celebrate the two hundred twelfth anniversary of Juan Pablo Duarte's birth. Juan Pablo Duarte worked with Francisco Del Rosario Sanchez and Ramon Matias Mella to form La Trinitaria. This was a secret organization that started the movement to make the Dominican Republic independent. Juan Pablo Duarte took part in the Dominican War of Independence and showed his commitment to his nation's freedom. He brought together military people, civilians, and financial supporters to work toward independence and self rule. Duarte went on a diplomatic mission to get help from Venezuela during the Dominican Restoration War from eighteen sixty three to eighteen sixty five. This helped the Dominican Republic regain its second independence from Spain. Duarte's liberal and democratic ideas helped create an independent Dominican state that was free from foreign control. Juan Pablo Duarte turned down requests from his supporters to become president of the Republic because he wanted to put democratic principles before his personal ambitions. Even though he faced exile, betrayal, and personal loss, Duarte put the welfare of the Dominican Republic and its people first. During February twenty twenty five, Dominican Americans and people in the Dominican Republic will celebrate the independence of the Dominican Republic. This includes attending the eighth annual Dominicans on the Hill conference.

sections.title: House Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports the goals and ideals of Juan Pablo Duarte Day of the Dominican Republic. The House supports creating a national day of recognition for Juan Pablo Duarte in the United States to honor the founding father of the Dominican Republic. The House urges the people of the United States to observe a national day of recognition for Dominican Americans with appropriate ceremonies, programs, and activities.
